---
title: "Environmental Systems & Geospatial References"
subtitle: "Earth Data Analytics, Mapping Packages, and Data Cubes"
author: "Brian Yandell ([_byandell.github.io_](https://byandell.github.io))"
date: last-modified
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, custom.scss]
    transition: slide
    slide-number: true
    show-slide-number: all
    embed-resources: true
    footer: "Environmental Systems & Geospatial References"
    highlight-style: github
---

# Earth Data Analytics & Setup {background-color="#e0f2fe"}

## Earth Data Analytics (EDA) Foundations {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
### ESIIL & EarthLab Programs
- **[Fundamentals of EDA](https://cu-esiil-edu.github.io/esiil-learning-portal/foundations/pages/00-course-overviews/foundations/01-fundamentals/00-home.html)**:
  - 1. Open reproducible science.
  - 2. Time-series data analysis.
  - 3. Geospatial vector data.
  - 4. Final project (raster data).
- **[Introduction to Earth Data Science](https://www.earthdatascience.org/courses/intro-to-earth-data-science/)**:
  - [Syllabus Wiki](https://github.com/earthlab-education/Earth-Analytics-AY24/wiki).
  - [EDA Foundations Textbook](https://cu-esiil-edu.github.io/esiil-learning-portal/foundations/pages/00-course-overviews/foundations/00-home.html).
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
### Key References
- **[Mapping Inequality](https://dsl.richmond.edu/panorama/redlining/)**: Historical redlining and spatial disparity resources.
- **[Python Strategy](../python/)**: Related programming strategy.
- **[landmapy Package](https://github.com/byandell-envsys/landmapy)**: Helper tools for environmental coding.
:::
:::

## Local Environment Setup {.compact-slide}

### Required Tools
- **[Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/download)**: Primary IDE.
- **[Git Version Control](https://git-scm.com/downloads)**: Already included in tools like RStudio, or install separately.
- **[Miniconda](https://docs.anaconda.com/miniconda/)**: Lightweight Conda package manager.
  - For macOS (M1/M2 Apple Silicon), download and run the Apple Silicon bash installer.

### Conda Terminal Commands
Run the following commands in the terminal to set up Miniconda:
```bash
# Create target directory
mkdir -p ~/miniconda3

# Download Apple Silicon installer
curl https://repo.anaconda.com/miniconda/Miniconda3-latest-MacOSX-arm64.sh -o ~/miniconda3/miniconda.sh

# Run bash installation script
bash ~/miniconda3/miniconda.sh -b -u -p ~/miniconda3

# Clean up installer script
rm ~/miniconda3/miniconda.sh
```
See further setup steps in [Git, Conda & Bash Setup](https://www.earthdatascience.org/workshops/setup-earth-analytics-python/setup-git-bash-conda/).

## GitHub SSH Key Configuration {.compact-slide}

Securely connect your local terminal to GitHub using SSH authentication:

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
### Step 1: Generate an SSH Key
Open your terminal and run the key-generator:
```bash
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "byandell@wisc.edu"
```
Press enter to accept the default storage location:
`~/.ssh/id_ed25519`
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
### Step 2: Copy Public Key
Copy the key to your clipboard using macOS `pbcopy`:
```bash
cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub | pbcopy
# or alternative pipe syntax
pbcopy < ~/.ssh/id_ed25519.pub
```
Add the copied key under GitHub settings.
:::
:::

::: {.callout-note}
For troubleshooting, see GitHub's guide on [Generating SSH Keys](https://docs.github.com/en/authentication/connecting-to-github-with-ssh/generating-a-new-ssh-key-and-adding-it-to-the-ssh-agent).
:::

# Mapping Platforms & R APIs {background-color="#e0f2fe"}

## Open Street Map (OSM) in R {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}
### OSM Ecosystem
- **[OpenStreetMap](https://www.openstreetmap.org/)**: Free, crowdsourced maps and spatial layers.
- **`osmdata` package**: Access and download features (pois, polygons, highways) directly inside R.
- **R vs Python**: While Python is highly popular for GIS, R has robust mapping integration.
:::

::: {.column width="40%"}
::: {.callout-tip}
### R Plotting Workflow
- Plot vector overlays with
[ggplot2](https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org)
+
[sf](https://r-spatial.github.io/sf/)
using
[geom_sf](https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org/reference/ggsf.html)
- Add basemaps with
[annotation_map_tile](https://paleolimbot.github.io/ggspatial/reference/annotation_map_tile.html)
from the
[ggspatial](https://paleolimbot.github.io/ggspatial/)
package.
:::
:::
:::

### Documentation & Guides:
- [OSM Contribution Wiki](https://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/How_to_contribute)
- [osmdata Vignette](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/osmdata/vignettes/osmdata.html)
- [Mapping OSM in R (JColiver)](https://jcoliver.github.io/learn-r/017-open-street-map.html)
- [ggspatial documentation](https://paleolimbot.github.io/ggspatial/)

## Thematic & Interactive Mapping {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
### Interactive tmap
- **`tmap` package**: Thematic map rendering with static/interactive modes.
- Toggle between plot mode and interactive viewer.
- Use `make-valid` function from `sf` package to solve multipolygon rendering errors.
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
### Useful Resources
- [tmap Quickstart](https://r-tmap.github.io/tmap/): Thematic maps in R.
- [tmap book](https://r-tmap.github.io/tmap-book/): Advanced visual layouts.
- [Making Maps with R](https://bookdown.org/nicohahn/making_maps_with_r5/docs/introduction.html).
- [Multipolygon Fixes](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/76455486/impossible-to-plot-osm-multipolygons-in-tmap-and-leaflet) on Stack Overflow.
:::
:::

::: {.callout-note}
For automated shapefile processing, see [Automating maps with GeoPandas and Matplotlib](https://medium.com/@sooryanarayan_5231/automating-map-generation-from-multi-polygon-shapefiles-using-python-with-geopandas-and-matplotlib-aad4c79f8d5e).
:::

## Google Maps Platform Access {.compact-slide}

Google Maps layers are accessible in R via the `ggmap` package, but require strict authentication:

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
### API Key Setup
1. Register on the [Google Maps Platform](https://mapsplatform.google.com/).
2. Enable the **Maps Static API**.
3. Generate and secure your API Key.
4. Note that Google Maps requires billing setup (credit card).
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
### Integration Code
Register the key inside your R script:
```R
library(ggmap)
register_google(key = "YOUR_API_KEY")
```

### Institutional Guidelines
- [Get Static Maps API Key](https://developers.google.com/maps/documentation/maps-static/get-api-key/).
- [UW-Madison Guidelines on Google Maps Embedding](https://wiscweb.wisc.edu/2018/11/30/events-calendar-embedded-map-display-changes/) ⚠️.
:::
:::

# Geospatial Packages & Resources {background-color="#e0f2fe"}

## Books & Online Literature {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
### Data Carpentry Workshops
- **[Intro to R for Geospatial Data](https://datacarpentry.org/r-intro-geospatial/)**
- **[Intro to Geospatial Concepts](https://datacarpentry.org/organization-geospatial/)**
- **[Geospatial Raster/Vector with R](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/r-raster-vector-geospatial/)**
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
### Key Books & Discussions
- **[Spatial Data Science (Pebesma & Bivand)](https://r-spatial.org/book/)**: Core text for R spatial analysis.
- **[Geospatial Vector with sf (Coding Club)](https://ourcodingclub.github.io/tutorials/spatial-vector-sf/)**.
- **[stars vs terra package comparison](https://www.seascapemodels.org/rstats/2021/06/01/STARS.html) ⚠️**.
:::
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
### Stack Overflow Tips
See threads like [stack geotiff with stars along](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/75249639/stack-geotiff-with-stars-along-when-band-dimension-contains-band-time-info) to handle complex band + time dimensions.
:::

## Desktop GIS & Integration {.compact-slide}

For advanced geospatial processing, combine R scripts with desktop GIS interfaces:

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### QGIS Integrations
- **QGIS**: Open-source desktop GIS.
- **[R to QGIS workflows](https://rpubs.com/DUE-methods1/r-qgis)**.
- **[Spatial Stratified Sampling](https://bookdown.org/einavg7/sp_technical_guide/spatial-stratified-sampling-with-rstudio-and-qgis.html)**.
- **[RQGIS package](https://dges.carleton.ca/CUOSGwiki/index.php/RQGIS,_Utilizing_Rstudio_as_an_alternative_GIS)**: Interface QGIS functions directly from RStudio.
- **[Processing lidar data](https://rstudio-pubs-static.s3.amazonaws.com/230154_30a0bbf22e2a49ecbfa1b72b2c7a8f96.html)** using QGIS, LAStools, and R.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Other Platforms & Guides
- **[ArcGIS Online](https://www.arcgis.com/sharing/rest/oauth2/signup?client_id=arcgisonline&response_type=token) ⚠️**: Free accounts available for online map sharing and storage.
- **[Land cover change mapping](https://www.unescap.org/resources/producing-land-cover-change-maps-and-statistics-step-step-guide-use-qgis-and-rstudio)** using QGIS and RStudio.
- **[Columbia Library Guide](https://guides.library.columbia.edu/geotools/R)** on GIS & Cartography tools in R.
:::
:::

## R Geospatial Packages {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Data Cube Creation
- **`gdalcubes`**: Form Earth observation data cubes from satellite image collections (e.g. MODIS).
- **`sf`**: Modern vector processing (replacing `sp`).
- **`stars`**: Spatiotemporal arrays, raster data cubes.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Raster & Tidy Extensions
- **`terra`**: Modern raster & vector processing (replacing legacy `raster` package).
- **`tidyterra`**: Extends standard `tidyverse` methods (like ggplot, filter, mutate) to `terra` raster and vector objects.
:::
:::

> [!WARNING]
> The legacy geospatial package `rgdal` is now obsolete and has been retired. Developers should transition all workflows to `sf` and `terra`.

# Repositories & Workshops {background-color="#e0f2fe"}

## Geospatial APIs & Repositories {.compact-slide}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### Download APIs
- **`rstac`**: Search and download satellite data via SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog (STAC) API.
- **`osmdata`**: Retrieve OpenStreetMap coordinates.
- **`geos`**: R API wrapper for the GEOS geometry engine.
- **`landsat`**: Process Landsat multispectral imagery.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### Repositories
- **[ESIIL Data Library](https://data-library.esiil.org) ⚠️**:
  - Features [American Indian & Alaska Native Areas](https://data-library.esiil.org/indian_country/aiannh/AIANNH/) ⚠️ datasets.
- **[ESIIL Flood Data](https://cu-esiil.github.io/hackathon2023_datacube/code_for_building_cube/Pull_flood_data/)**: Flood and moisture layers.
- **[datasets.csv](https://github.com/byandell/geospatial/blob/main/datasets.csv)**: Compiled dataset list.
:::
:::

## Coordinate Projections & Datasets {.compact-slide}

Geospatial data use different Coordinate Reference Systems (CRS). Coordinate transformation is crucial:

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}
### Key Reference Projections
- **[EPSG:4326](https://epsg.io/4326)**: WGS84 - World Geodetic System 1984 (lat/lon coordinates).
- **[EPSG:32618](https://epsg.io/32618)**: WGS84 for UTM zone 18N (North America projections).
- **[EPSG:32730](https://epsg.io/32720)**: WGS84 for UTM zone 20S (South America projections).
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
### NEON Project Areas
Geospatial workshop datasets focus on two primary field sites:
- **Harvard Forest (HARV)**:
  - `HARV_dsmCrop.tif` (surface model).
  - `HARV_DSMhill.tif` (hillshade model).
- **San Joaquin Range (SJER)**.
:::
:::

::: {.callout-note}
Data is hosted on FigShare. See the [NEON Raster Introduction](https://www.neonscience.org/resources/learning-hub/tutorials/dc-raster-data-r) for raster tutorials in R.
:::

## Geospatial Workshops {.compact-slide}

Interactive worksheets and hackathons are excellent starting points for hands-on learning:

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
### ESIIL Hackathon 2023
- Focus: Creating Datacubes.
- **[The Art of Making a Datacube](https://cu-esiil.github.io/hackathon2023_datacube/)**.
- **[ESIIL_Art_Data_Cube.Rmd](https://github.com/byandell/geospatial/blob/main/ESIIL_Art_Data_Cube.Rmd)**: Edit of Ty Tuff's datacube creation methods.
- Mounted STAC dataset access and on-the-fly downloads.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
### UW-Madison Workshop 2023
- **[Data Carpentry Workshop](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/2023-06-05-uwmadison-dc/)**.
- **[Carpentries Etherpad](https://pad.carpentries.org/2023-06-05-uwmadison-dc)**: Collaborative notes.
- **[Geospatial.Rmd](https://github.com/byandell/geospatial/blob/main/Geospatial.Rmd)**: Rmarkdown compilation of workshop exercises.
:::
:::
